title: How to book 2-5 meetings a day on LinkedIn with Claude Sonnet 5
author: Kakiyo / Ilan Asseo
steals: 8-prompt architecture for autonomous LinkedIn outreach: Build ICP → Capture Voice → Research Prospect → Write Icebreaker → Run Conversation → Handle Objections → Book Signal → Revive Cold
Conversation Design methodology: give the AI instructions on HOW to hold a conversation, not rigid templates
30-40% reply rate claim vs 5-10% for template sequences — difference is personalization + agentic conversation
Signal-based lead targeting: engagers on your content, competitors' audience, job changes, raises
Sonnet 5 is agentic enough to run the entire conversation loop autonomously — find, write, reply, book
Voice profile as persistent context: paste real writings → AI builds a voice fingerprint for all subsequent messages
Objection lab prompt: read the REAL objection behind their words (skepticism vs trust vs priority vs fit)
Booking signal checklist: audit run? acknowledged issues? asked how much? expressed frustration? no budget constraints?
Revive cadence: reference previous conversation + add new value + never apologize for following up
Full autopilot: define targets once, set messaging style, test, turn on — everything else runs itself
nebula_adaptation: Created growth_system/outreach_prompt_architecture.md (10.5k chars) — full 8-prompt chain adapted for Nebula's stack. Each prompt maps to existing assets (Voice DNA, cold_email_frameworks, reply_templates, ICP_MEMO) with gaps identified. Bottleneck analysis: Nebula's cruns run on Sonnet 4 — Sonnet 5 needed for autonomous conversation loop. Updated email-sequences cron prompt to reference the 8-prompt architecture.
